zh.lifeSummary: 當Mattias Nilsson Kagg於1502年出生在瑞典索德曼藍德費亞爾斯亞福特時，他的父親Nils Erlandsson Kagge47歲，母親Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht38歲。他於大約1530年與Elin Lindormsdotter在瑞典索德曼藍德福洛達結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1568年在他的家鄉享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mattias Nilsson Kagg于1502年出生在瑞典索德曼藍德費亞爾斯亞福特时，他的父亲Nils Erlandsson Kagge47岁，母亲Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht38岁。他于大约1530年与Elin Lindormsdotter在瑞典索德曼藍德福洛達结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1568年在他的家乡享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Nilsson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Nils, Per, Anders, Egil, Lennart, Sven, Birgit, Fredrik, Sten, Algot.  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Gerhard, Gotthard, Karl-Heinz.
Swedish and German: patronymic from the personal name Nils, a shortened form of Nikolaus. Compare Nilson , Neilson 3, and Nielson 2.

variants.name: Nilson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Nils, Erik, Alvar, Holger, Kristoffer, Mats, Sven.
Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish   Nilsson  .
Americanized form of Norwegian   Nilsen   and of Danish, Norwegian, or North German   Nielsen  , all cognates of 1. Compare   Nielson  .

nameMeanings.name: Kagg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Yong, Jung, Shin, Sang, Kyung, Hyun, Sun, Myung, Soon, Jae.  Korean Chang, Min, Dae, Myong, Moon, Byung, Chong, Seong, Jeong, Chung, Young Woo, Chul.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 康, meaning ‘healthy’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of Kang Shu (康叔), the posthumous title of one of the sons of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056  BC ). Kang Shu was the first king of the state of Wey (衛) (which is located mainly around present-day Hebi and Xinxiang in Henan province). (ii) from the first element of Kang Ju (康居), the name of a state in Central Asia during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). People from this state adopted 康 as their surname. (iii) from Kang (康), the Chinese name of the state of Samarkand in Central Asia. Immigrants from Samarkand in China acquired 康, the Chinese name of their state as their surname, known as one of the 'Nine Sogdian Surnames’. (iv) adopted in place of another Chinese surname, 匡 (see   Kuang   2). Some people with the surname 匡 changed their surname to 康 during the reign of Zhao Kuangyin (赵匡胤), known as Emperor Taizu of Song (927–976  AD ), the first emperor of the Northern Song dynasty (960–1127  AD ), because 匡 happened to be part of the personal name of the emperor, which was taboo in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 亢, meaning ‘high’ or ‘haughty’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the placename Kang Fu (亢父), the name of a vassal state (in present-day Jining in Shandong province) in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted in place of another Chinese surname, 伉 (Kang in Mandarin pinyin), also sharing the same origin of another two Chinese surnames 抗 (Kang in Mandarin pinyin) and 杭 (pronounced the same as 抗 in ancient Chinese, Hang in today's Mandarin pinyin), which can be traced back to San Kang (三伉/抗/杭), a noble official in the state of Wey (衛) during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ).

variants.name: Kuang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Jian, Mei, Fu, Guang, Guo, Guo Rong, Hong, Hui, Jian Ming, Lei, Li, Ping.

Mandarin form of the surname 鄺: (i) from Kuang (鄺, also written as 曠), the name of an ancient state (probably located in present-day Anhui province). (ii) said to be adopted in place of the surname 黃 by the sons of Huang Xian, a general during the reign of Emperor Zhongzong of Tang (656–710  AD ). It was said that Huang Xian's two sons changed their surname 黃 (Huang) to 曠 (Kuang) and 鄺 (Kuang) in order to escape from political persecutions because their father Huang Xian lost the battle to the invaders on the frontier. (iii) said to be traced back to Fang Zhun, an official during the Southern Song dynasty (1127  AD  – 1279  AD ), who was endowed with the surname Kuang (鄺) by the then emperor because Zhun's daughter became the emperor's consort. He was later demoted and moved to Heyuan (located in present-day Guangdong province).

variants.name: Khang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Ying, Leng, Nhia, Chue, Sung, Tong, Yang, Chee, Chi, Chia, Chin, Chou, Neng, Pang, Shoua, Yer, Youa, Blong, Chong, Chong Neng, Chung, Houa, Khoua, Moua, Khue, Mai, Nguyen, Tran.

Southeast Asian (Hmong): from the name of the Khang or Kha clan of the Hmong people in Laos, China, and Vietnam; in Chinese characters it is written 康 (see   Kang   1). Compare   Kha   2 and   Kang   8.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 康, see   Kang   1.

nameMeanings.name: Mattias
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian common variant spelling of   Mathias .

historicEvents.title: The Stockholm Bloodbath
historicEvents.description: A mass execution of Swedish nobles by Danish king Christian II is known as the Stockholm Bloodbath. Christian II invaded Sweden in 1519 and took hold of Stockholm in September 1520. Over 80 nobles were executed for heresy on November 8 and 9. The outrage from the executions led to the Swedish removal of support of the Kalmar Union.

historicEvents.title: Swedish War of Liberation
historicEvents.description: The Swedish War of Liberation was a civil war led by nobleman Gustav Vasa. As Christian II heard of the rebellion, he confronted Vasa's men, who won the battle at Brunbacks Ferry. Christian II was defeated by the Swedes in Stockholm and a truce was agreed upon. Vasa was given as a hostage as a show of good faith until the treaty was signed. The Danish King did not keep his word and Vasa was held as a prisoner for two years until his escape. A bounty was placed on Vasa's head. Vasa worked to inspire many to join his cause and lived under disguise until the Danes surrendered in 1525.

historicEvents.title: First Full Swedish Translation of the Bible
historicEvents.description: The first full Swedish translation of the Bible, known as the Gustav Bible, was completed by Olaus Petri and Laurentius Andreae and published in 1540.

historicEvents.title: Sweden Becomes Lutheran Nation
historicEvents.description: Sweden officially declared itself a Lutheran nation in 1544.

en.lifeSummary: When Mattias Nilsson Kagg was born in 1502, in Fjällskäfte, Södermanland, Sweden, his father, Nils Erlandsson Kagge, was 47 and his mother, Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht, was 38. He married Elin Lindormsdotter about 1530, in Floda, Södermanland, Sweden. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died in 1568, in his hometown, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mattias Nilsson Kagg est né en 1502, à Fjällskäfte, Södermanland, Suède, son père, Nils Erlandsson Kagge, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Elin Lindormsdotter environ 1530, à Floda, Södermanland, Suède. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1568, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mattias Nilsson Kagg 1502 in Fjällskäfte, Södermanland, Schweden geboren wurde, war sein Vater Nils Erlandsson Kagge 47 und seine Mutter Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht 38. Er hat um 1530 in Floda, Södermanland, Schweden Elin Lindormsdotter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1568 im Alter von 66 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mattias Nilsson Kagg è nato il nel 1502 nel Fjällskäfte, Södermanland, Svezia, il suo padre, Nils Erlandsson Kagge, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Elin Lindormsdotter circa 1530, nel Floda, Södermanland, Svezia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1568, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Mattias Nilsson Kaggが1502年にスウェーデンスoデルマンランドフジaルスクaフトで生まれた時，父Nils Erlandsson Kaggeは47歳，母Anna Pedersdotter Örnflychtは38歳であった。約1530年にスウェーデンスoデルマンランドフロダでElin Lindormsdotterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1568年に故郷で66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mattias Nilsson Kagg(이)가 1502년 스웨덴 스o더먼랜드 프ja스ka프트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nils Erlandsson Kagge(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht(은)는 38세였다. 그는 대략 1530년 스웨덴 스o더먼랜드 플로다에서 Elin Lindormsdotter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1568년 그의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mattias Nilsson Kagg nasceu em 1502, em Fjällskäfte, Södermanland, Suécia, seu pai, Nils Erlandsson Kagge, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Elin Lindormsdotter aproximadamente 1530, em Floda, Södermanland, Suécia. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1568, em sua cidade natal, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mattias Nilsson Kagg родился в 1502 г. в местности фджaллскaфт, сoдирманланд, Швеция, его отцу по имени Nils Erlandsson Kagge было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Elin Lindormsdotter около 1530 г. в местности флода, сoдирманланд, Швеция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1568 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mattias Nilsson Kagg nació en 1502, en Fjällskäfte, Södermanland, Suecia, su papá, Nils Erlandsson Kagge, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Anna Pedersdotter Örnflycht, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Elin Lindormsdotter aproximadamente 1530, en Floda, Södermanland, Suecia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1568, en su pueblo, a la edad de 66 años.
